title: Water Resources Management (Groundwater and Boreholes) Regulations, 2018.

sections.heading: These Regulations may be cited as the Water Resources Title
sections.text: Management (Groundwater and Boreholes) Regulations, 2018.

sections.heading: In these Regulations unless context otherwise requires- Interpretation
sections.text:
"Authority" has the meaning assigned to the word in the
Act;
"brackish" has the meaning assigned to the word in the Act;
"casing" means a pipe of steel, unplasticised polyvinyl chloride
or any other suitable material inserted into a borehole to
support the borehole against collapse;
"drillers" has the meaning assigned to the word in the Act;
"gravel pack" means sand or gravel that is uniform, clean,
well rounded and siliceous;
"karst" means a Ii mestone rock layer that contains the cavities
and cracks, which are characterised by high permeability
and water movement is not homogeneous;
"pollution" has the meaning assigned to the word in the Act;
"pumping test" means a test carried out to assess the aquifer
or borehole characteristics;
Copies of this Statuto1y instrument can be oblainedjiwn the Government Printe1'.
PO. Box 30136. 10/0/. Lusaka. PriceK32.00 each
360 Statutory Instruments 7th March, 2018
"sanitary seal" means a seal composed ofa cement grout,
with which the annular space between the borehole
wall and the surface casing is filled in order to
prevent contamination of the borehole;
"screen" means a perforated section ofa casing pipe that
allows water to enter the borehole whilst restricting
sediments from entering the borehole;
"successful borehole" means a borehole that has acceptable
yield upon completion;
"Total Dissolved Sol ids" means the quantity of dissolved
material in water expressed as milligrams per litre;
and
"unsuccessful borehole" means a borehole that does not
yield any water or meet the acceptable yield.
Notice to 3. (1) A person who intends to drill a borehole shall give
drill notice of intention to drill a borehole to the Authority, catchment
borehole
council or subcatchment council in Form I set out in the First
Schedule.
(2) An application to drill a borehole sh�1I be made to the
Authority in Form II set out in the First Schedule, on payment of
the prescribed fee.
(3) The Authority shall, where it authorises the drilling of a
borehole, inform the applicant in Form III set out in the First
Schedule.
(4 ) The Authority shall, where it rejects the notification ofd rilling
a borehole, inform the applicant in Form IV set out in the First
Schedule.
Location of 4. (1) A driller shall ensure that the location of the borehole
boreholes meets the distances specified in the Second Schedule.
(2) Where the specified distances in the second Schedule are
not practicable, the Authority may approve such other distances.

sections.heading: (1) A driller shall ensure that a successful borehole is
sections.text:
Specification
of boreholes installed with the following:
(i) full casing of the borehole from the top to the bottom;
(ii) gravel pack;
(iii) bottom plug; and
(iv) a sanitary seal.
(2) An unsuccessful or exploratory borehole shall not require
the specifications under su bregulation (I).
7th March, 20 I 8 Statutory Instruments 361
(3) A driller shall ensure that an unsuccessful borehole 1s
backfilled and capped with grout within twentyfour hours.

sections.heading: (1) A person who drills a borehole shall register the borehole Register of
sections.text:
in Form V set out in the First Schedule, on payment ofa prescribed boreholes
fee.
(2) The Authority shall keep and maintain a Register of boreholes
as set out in the Third Schedule.

sections.heading: A person who owns a defective borehole shall repoti to the Defective
sections.text:
borehole
Authority or a catchment manager within fourteen days of
discovering brackish water or any other substance in Form VI set
out in the First Schedule.

sections.heading: The fees set out in the Water Resources Management (Fees Fees
sections.text:
and Charges) Regulations, 2018, are the applicable fees for the
purposes oft hese Regulations.

sections.heading: A person who contravenes these Regulations commits an General
sections.text:
offence and is liable, on conviction, to a fine not exceeding one. offence and
penalty
hundred thousand penalty units or to imprisonment for a term n�t
exceeding twelve months, or to both.
I 0. A person who had a borehole before the coming into Transitional
operation oft hese Regulations, shall provisions
register that borehole on payment of the prescribed fee within six
months of the coming into effect of these Regulations.

NOTICE OF GRANT TO DRILL BOREHOLE
To: ..................................................................................................................................... .
IN THE MATTER OF:. ............................................................................... ·····················
1._;
You are hereby notified that your application to drill has been accepted on the following
conditions:
(i) This permit is contingent on land ownership and is, therefore, transferable upon
ownership transfer, but is restricted to its conditions or any other relevant rules and
conditions prevalent at the time of transfer
(ii) Where there is transfer ofp roperty, the new property owner shall notify the Authority
in writing within ninety days.
(iii) In case where the applicant intends to drill more than one borehole, the applicant
shall
be required to fill in an application form for each borehole.
(iv) The borehole shall be drilled by a licensed driller in accordance with the Authority's
technical specifications.
(v) Upon completion of drilling a borehole, a driller shall be required to submit to the
Authority, a borehole completion report and any other tests made.
(vi) This permit is valid for six months but may be revoked at any stage due to
contravention
of any of the above stated conditions.
